name: jobbergate-cli
documentation:
# Jobbergate CLI

The Jobbergate CLI provides a command-line interface to view and manage the Jobbergate
resources. It can be used to create Job Scripts from template and then submit them to
the Slurm cluster to which Jobbergate is connected.

Jobbergate CLI is a Python project implemented with the
[Typer](https://typer.tiangolo.com/) CLI builder library. Its dependencies and
environment are managed by [Poetry](https://python-poetry.org/).

The CLI has a rich help system that can be accessed by passing the `--help` flag to
the main command:

```shell
jobbergate job-scripts --help
```

There is also help and parameter guides for each of the subcommands that can be accessed
by passing them the `--help` flag:

```shell
jobbergate job-scripts list --help
```

See also:

* [jobbergate-api](https://github.com/omnivector-solutions/jobbergate/jobbergate-api)

## License

* [MIT](./LICENSE)

## Copyright

* Copyright (c) 2020 OmniVector Solutions <info@omnivector.solutions>

The authentication handler for the context.

This is a cached property to ensure that the handler is only created when needed,
so commands that require no authentication face no configuration errors.

documentation:
Welcome to the Jobbergate CLI!

More information can be shown for each command listed below by running it with the --help option.

documentation: Logs out of the jobbergate-cli. Clears the saved user credentials.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.main.show_token
documentation:
Show the token for the logged in user.

Token output is automatically copied to your clipboard.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.main.safe_entrypoint
documentation:
Entrypoint for the app including custom error handling.

With this we ensure error handling is applied to all commands with no need
to duplicate the decorators on each of them.

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_submissions.tools.JobSubmissionABC.run
documentation: Run all required steps to create the job submission.

A dataclass representing a job submission for Jobbergate.

Args:

: jg_ctx: The JobbergateContext. Used to retrieve the client for requests and the email of the submitting user.
  job_script_id: The `id` of the Job Script to submit to Slurm.
  name: The name to attach to the Job Submission.
  description: An optional description that may be added to the Job Submission.
  cluster_name: An optional cluster_name for the cluster where the job should be executed.

  > If left off, it will default to the DEFAULT_CLUSTER_NAME from the settings.
  > If no default is set, an exception will be raised.

  execution_directory: An optional directory where the job should be executed. If provided as a

  : relative path, it will be constructed as an absolute path relative to
    the current working directory.

  download: A flag indicating whether the job script files should be downloaded to the execution directory.
  sbatch_arguments: An optional list of arguments to pass to inject into the job script.

The format returned is: [job ID, 8 chars] [job name]

documentation:
Return a list of input files in a directory that match a search term.

Ignore casing when comparing against the search term.

Default to searching for all files in the current directory.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.tools.load_default_config

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.tools.fetch_application_data_locally
documentation:
Retrieve an application from a local directory.

Args:
    application_path: The directory containing the application files.

Returns:
    A LocalApplication instance containing the application data.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.tools.fetch_application_data
documentation:
Retrieve an application from the API by id or identifier.

Args:
    jg_ctx: The JobbergateContext. Needed to access the Httpx client with which to make API calls.
    id: The id of the application to fetch.
    identifier: If supplied, look for an application instance with the provided identifier.

Returns:
    An instance of ApplicationResponse containing the application data.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.tools.load_application_data
documentation:
Validates and loads the data for an application returned from the API's applications GET endpoint.

As part of the Jobbergate data restructure, sections of the legacy jobbergate.yaml
are now stored in different tables in the backend. This function reconstructs
them from app_data.workflow_file.runtime_config and app_data.template_vars
for backward compatibility.

Args:
    app_data: A dictionary containing the application data.
    application_source_file: The source file of the application.

Returns:
    A tuple containing the application config and the application module.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.tools.get_upload_files
documentation:
Context manager to build the files parameter.

Open the supplied file(s) and build a files param appropriate for using
multi-part file uploads with the client.

Args:
    application_path: The directory where the application files are located.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.tools.upload_application
documentation:
Upload an application given an application path and the application id.

Args:
    jg_ctx: The JobbergateContext. Needed to access the Httpx client with which to make API calls.
    application_path: The directory where the application files to upload may be found.
    application_id: The id of the application for which to upload data.
    application_identifier: The identifier of the application for which to upload data.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.tools.save_application_files
documentation: Save the application files from the API response into a local destination.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.tools.load_application_config_from_source
documentation:
Load the JobbergateApplicationConfig from a text string containing the config as YAML.

Args:
    config_source: The YAML containing the config

Returns:
    A JobbergateApplicationConfig instance with the config values

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.tools.load_application_from_source
documentation:
Load the JobbergateApplication class from a text string containing the source file.

Creates the module in a temporary file and imports it with importlib.

Adapted from: https://docs.python.org/3/library/importlib.html#importing-a-source-file-directly

Args:
    app_source: The JobbergateApplication source code to load
    app_config: The JobbergateApplicationConfig needed to instantiate the JobbergateApplication

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.tools.ApplicationRuntime._update_template_files_information
documentation: Update the information about the template files if not already present in the configuration.

Prepare and execute a Jobbergate application gathering the answers to the questions.

Args:
    app_data: The application data, can be either an ApplicationResponse or a LocalApplication.
    app_source_code: The source code of the application, often coming from jobbergate.py file.
    supplied_params: The parameters supplied to the application, defaults to an empty dictionary.
    fast_mode: A flag indicating whether the application is in fast mode, defaults to False.

modules.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions
documentation:
Abstraction layer for questions. Each class represents different question types.

The questions describe literal questions that are asked of the user in an interactive
mode via the `inquirer` package.

Questions will be skipped and use the default value if the `ignore` property resolves
to True.

Questions will also resolve to their default values if running in "fast mode".

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.QuestionBase

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.QuestionBase.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Question.

Args:

: variablename: The key in the config dictionary that this question will set.
  message: The message to show the user that describes what the question is gathering.
  ignore: If true, do not ask the question and just use the default value instead.
  default: The default value for the variablename in the answers dict.
  inquirer_type: The `inquirer` question type that this `QuestionBase` wraps.

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.QuestionBase.make_prompts
documentation:
Create `inquirer` prompts from this instance of `QuestionBase`.

Args:

: override_kwargs: A collection of keyword arguments to override in initializing the `inquirer` question.

Baseclass for questions.

All questions have variablename, message and an optional default.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.Text
documentation: Asks for a text value.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.Integer

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.Integer.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Integer question.

Args:
    variablename: The key in the config dictionary that this question will set.
    message: The message to show the user that describes what the question is gathering.
    minval: The minimum value the integer may be set to. If not specified, use negative infinity.
    maxval: The maximum value the integer may be set to. If not specified, use infinity.

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.Integer._validator
documentation: Provide a custom validator that checks the value of the integer to make sure it is in range.
Asks for an integer value. Could have min and/or max constrains.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.List

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.List.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the List question.

Args:
    variablename: The key in the config dictionary that this question will set.
    message: The message to show the user that describes what the question is gathering.
    choices: A list of the possible values from which the Question will allow the user to select one.
Gives the user a list to choose one from.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.Directory

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.Directory.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Directory question.

Args:
    variablename: The key in the config dictionary that this question will set.
    message: The message to show the user that describes what the question is gathering.
    exists: If True, ensure that the directory exists on the system.

Asks for a directory name. If `exists` is `True`, it checks if the path exists and is a directory.

Args:
    exists: Checks if the given directory exists.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.File

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.File.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the File question.

Args:
    variablename: The key in the config dictionary that this question will set.
    message: The message to show the user that describes what the question is gathering.
    exists: If True, ensure that the file path exists on the system.
Asks for a file name.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.Checkbox

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.Checkbox.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Checkbox question.

Args:
    variablename: The key in the config dictionary that this question will set.
    message: The message to show the user that describes what the question is gathering.
    choices: A list of the possible values from which the Question will allow the user to select many.
Gives the user a list to choose multiple entries from.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.Confirm

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.Confirm.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Confirm question.

Args:
    variablename: The key in the config dictionary that this question will set.
    message: The message to show the user that describes what the question is gathering.
Asks a question with a boolean answer (true/false).

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.BooleanList

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.BooleanList.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Checkbox question.

Args:
    variablename: The key in the config dictionary that this question will set.
    message: The message to show the user that describes what the question is gathering.
    whentrue: List of questions to ask if the user answers 'true' on this question.
    whenfalse: List of questions to show if the user answers 'false' on this question.

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.BooleanList.ignore_child
documentation:
Dynamically check if a child question should be ignored based on the questions that have already been answered.

Args:
    child: The child question that might be ignored.
    answers: Answer values to previously asked questions.

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.BooleanList.make_ignore_partial
documentation:
Build a partial method for checking if a child should be ignored.

This method just makes the code more readable so that a non-descriptive lambda does not need to be used inline.

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.BooleanList.make_prompts
documentation:
Create `inquirer` prompts from this instance of `BooleanList` and for all its child questions.

Args:

: override_kwargs: A collection of keyword arguments to override in the base `make_prompts` method.
Asks a confirmation question that is followed up by a certain question list when true and a different list if false.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.Const

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.Const.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Const "question".

Args:
    variablename: The key in the config dictionary that this question will set.

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.applications.questions.Const.make_prompts
documentation: Create `inquirer` prompts from this instance of `Const`.
Sets the variable to the `default` value. Doesn't show anything.

Provide a stub module to maintain compatibility with previous versions.

Issue a deprecation warning when this module is imported from if JOBBERGATE_COMPATIBILITY_MODE is enabled.

If JOBBERGATE_COMPATIBILITY_MODE is not enabled, raise an import error when this module is imported.

documentation: Provide tool functions for working with Job Script data

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.tools.validate_parameter_file
documentation:
Validate parameter file at the supplied path and returns the parsed dict.

Args:
    parameter_path: Path to the parameter file.

Raises:
    Abort: If file does not exist or is not valid JSON.

Returns:
    Parsed dictionary from the parameter file.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.tools.fetch_job_script_data
documentation: Retrieve a job_script from the API by ``id``

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.tools.remove_prefix
documentation: Remove the prefix 'templates/' from a string

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.tools.remove_prefix_suffix
documentation: Remove the prefix 'templates/' and suffixes '.j2' and '.jinja2' from a string

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.tools.get_template_output_name_mapping
documentation:
Get the mapping of template names to output names.

This provides the mapping as expected by the API v4 from the configuration on CLI v3.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.tools.inject_sbatch_params
documentation:
Inject sbatch params into job script.

Given the job script as job_script_data_as_string, inject the sbatch params in the correct location.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.tools.render_template
documentation:
Render a template file and save it to the output directory.

Args:
    template_path: The path to the template file.
    parameters: The parameters to use for rendering the template.

Returns:
    The rendered template as a string.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.tools.render_job_script_locally
documentation:
Render a new job script from an application in a local directory.

Args:
    jg_ctx: The Jobbergate context.
    job_script_name: Name of the new job script.
    application_path: Path to the base application.
    output_path: Path to the output the rendered job script.
    sbatch_params: List of sbatch parameters.
    param_file: Path to a parameters file.
    fast: Whether to use default answers (when available) instead of asking the user.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.tools.render_job_script
documentation:
Render a new job script from an application.

Args:
    jg_ctx: The Jobbergate context.
    name: Name of the new job script.
    application_id: Id of the base application.
    application_identifier: Identifier of the base application.
    description: Description of the new job script.
    sbatch_params: List of sbatch parameters.
    param_file: Path to a parameters file.
    fast: Whether to use default answers (when available) instead of asking the user.

Returns:
    The new job script.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.tools.upload_job_script_files
documentation:
Upload a job-script and its supporting files given their paths and the job-script id.

Args:

: jg_ctx: The `JobbergateContext` needed to access the Httpx client with which to make API calls
  job_script_path: The path to the job-script file to upload
  supporting_file_paths: The paths to any supporting files to upload with the job-scritpt
  job_script_id: The id of the job-script for which to upload data

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.tools.save_job_script_file
documentation: Save a job script file from the API response to the destination path.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.tools.download_job_script_files
documentation: Download all job script files from the API and save them to the destination path.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.tools.question_helper
documentation:
Helper function for asking questions to the user.

Args:
    question_func: The function to use to ask the question
    text: The text of the question to ask
    default: The default value to use if the user does not provide one
    fast: Whether to use default answers (when available) instead of asking the user
    actual_value: The actual value provided by the user, if any

Returns:
    `actual_value` or `default` or the value provided by the user

Notes:
    The `actual_value` has the most priority and will be returned if it is not None.
    After evaluating the `actual_value`, the fast mode will determine if the default value will be used.
    Otherwise, the question will be prompted to the user.

modules.name: jobbergate_cli.logging
documentation: Provide initializers for logging.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.logging.init_logs
documentation:
Initialize logging.

If JOBBERGATE_LOG_PATH is set in the config, add a rotating file log handler.
Logs will be retained for 1 week.

If verbose is supplied, add a stdout handler at the DEBUG level.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.logging.init_sentry
documentation: Initialize Sentry if the `SENTRY_DSN` environment variable is present.

documentation: Get a single job script by id.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.app.create_stand_alone
documentation: Create and upload files for a standalone job script (i.e., unrelated to any application).

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.app.create_locally
documentation:
Create a job-script from local application files (ideal for development and troubleshooting).

The templates will be overwritten with the rendered files.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.app.create
documentation: Create a new job script from an application.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.app.update
documentation: Update an existing job script.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.app.delete
documentation: Delete an existing job script.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.app.show_files
documentation: Show the files for a single job script by id.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.app.download_files
documentation: Download the files from a job script to the current working directory.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.subapps.job_scripts.app.clone
documentation: Clone an existing job script, so the user can own and modify a copy of it.

modules.name: jobbergate_cli.application_base
documentation:
Provide a stub module to maintain compatibility with previous versions.

Issue a deprecation warning when this module is imported from if JOBBERGATE_COMPATIBILITY_MODE is enabled.

If JOBBERGATE_COMPATIBILITY_MODE is not enabled, raise an import error when this module is imported.

modules.name: jobbergate_cli.auth
documentation: Utilities for handling auth in jobbergate-cli.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.auth.open_on_browser
documentation: Open the url on the browser using webbrowser.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.auth.show_login_message
documentation: Show a message to the user with a link to the auth provider to login.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.auth._show_login_narrow_screen
documentation: Print the link out of the panel to make it easier to copy.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.auth._show_login_standard_screen
documentation: Print a rich panel with a link to the auth provider to login.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.auth.track_login_progress
documentation: Track the progress of the login process on a progress bar.

modules.name: jobbergate_cli.text_tools
documentation: Provide some basic tools for manipulating text.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.text_tools.dedent
documentation: Dedents a paragraph after removing leading and trailing whitespace.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.text_tools.unwrap
documentation:
Unwraps a paragraph of text into a single line.

The text may be indented.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.text_tools.conjoin
documentation:
Joins strings supplied as args.

Helper that wraps `str.join()` without having to pack strings in an iterable.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.text_tools.dedent_all
documentation: Dedents each blob supplied as an argument and then joins them.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.text_tools.indent
documentation: Simple wrapper for the textwrap.indent() method but includes a default prefix.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.text_tools.copy_to_clipboard
documentation:
Copy the provided text to the clipboard.

If the clipboard is not available, return False. Otherwise, return True.

modules.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas
documentation: Provide Pydantic models for various data items.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.TokenSet
documentation: A model representing a pairing of access and refresh tokens

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.IdentityData
documentation: A model representing the identifying data for a user from an auth token.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.Persona
documentation:
A model representing a pairing of a TokenSet and user email.
This is a convenience to combine all of the identifying data and credentials for a given user.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.DeviceCodeData
documentation: A model representing the data that is returned from the OIDC provider's device code endpoint.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.ContextProtocol
documentation:
A protocol describing context passed from the main entry point.

It aims to help static type checkers at the same time that prevents
circular import issues on the actual implementation.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.JobbergateConfig

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.JobbergateConfig.compute_extra_settings
documentation: Compute missing values and extra operations to enhance the user experience and backward compatibility.

A data object describing the config values needed in the "jobbergate_config" section of the
JobbergateApplicationConfig model.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.JobbergateApplicationConfig
documentation: A data object describing the config data needed to instantiate a JobbergateApplication class.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.ApplicationResponse
documentation: Describes the format of data for applications retrieved from the Jobbergate API endpoints.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.LocalTemplateFile
documentation: Template file retrieved from a local folder.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.LocalWorkflowFile
documentation: Workflow file retrived from a local folder.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.LocalApplication
documentation: Application retrieved from a local folder.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.JobScriptFile
documentation: Model containing job-script files.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.JobScriptResponse

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.JobScriptResponse.null_files
documentation: Remap a `None` value in files to an empty list.
Describes the format of data for job_scripts retrieved from the Jobbergate API endpoints.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.JobSubmissionResponse
documentation: Describes the format of data for job_submissions retrieved from the Jobbergate API endpoints.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.JobScriptCreateRequest
documentation: Request model for creating JobScript instances.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.RenderFromTemplateRequest
documentation: Request model for creating a JobScript entry from a template.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.JobScriptRenderRequestData
documentation: Describes the data that will be sent to the `create` endpoint of the Jobbergate API for job scripts.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.JobSubmissionCreateRequestData
documentation: Describes the data that will be sent to the `create` endpoint of the Jobbergate API for job submissions.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.ListResponseEnvelope
documentation: A model describing the structure of response envelopes from "list" endpoints.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.schemas.ClusterCacheData
documentation: Describes the format of data stored in the clusters cache file.

modules.name: jobbergate_cli.requests
documentation: Provide utilities for making requests against the Jobbergate API.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.requests.get_possible_solution_to_error
documentation: Get a possible solution to an error code.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.requests.format_response_error
documentation: Format a response into a human-readable error message, including the cause, and a possible solution.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.requests._deserialize_request_model
documentation: Deserialize a pydantic model instance into request_kwargs for an httpx client request in place.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.requests.make_request
documentation:
Make a request against the Jobbergate API.

Args:
    client: The Httpx client to use for the request.
    url_path: The path to add to the base url of the client where the request should be sent.
    method: The REST method to use for the request (GET, PUT, UPDATE, POST, DELETE, etc).
    expected_status: The status code to expect on the response. If it is not received, raise an Abort.
    expect_response: Indicates if response data (JSON) is expected from the API endpoint.
    abort_message: The message to show the user if there is a problem and the app must be aborted.
    abort_subject: The subject to use in Abort output to the user.
    support: If true, add a message to the output instructing the user to seek help.
    response_model_cls: If supplied, serialize the response data into this Pydantic model class.
    request_model: Use a pydantic model instance as the data body for the request.
    save_to_file: If supplied, save the response data to this file.
    request_kwargs: Any additional keyword arguments to pass to the request.

Returns:
    The response from the API, either as a Pydantic model, a dictionary, or an integer status code.

modules.name: jobbergate_cli.config
documentation:
Configuration file, sets all the necessary environment variables.
Can load configuration from a dotenv file if supplied.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.config._get_env_file
documentation: Load the env file based on the constant JOBBERGATE_DEFAULT_DOTENV_PATH if the file exists. Otherwise use ".env".

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.config.build_settings
documentation: Return a Setting object and handle ValidationError with a message to the user.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.config.Settings

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.config.Settings._validate_cache_dir
documentation: Expand, resolve, and create cache directory.

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.config.Settings.is_onsite_mode
documentation: Check if the SBATCH_PATH is set, indicating that the CLI is running in on-site mode.
Provide a `pydantic` settings model to hold configuration values loaded from the environment.

modules.name: jobbergate_cli.compat
documentation:
Provide compatibility to the previous version of Jobbergate CLI for users who have automation or are
familiar with the old commands

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.compat.add_legacy_compatible_commands
documentation:
Add commands from the restructured CLI under the previous names for the commands
to the root `typer` app.

modules.name: jobbergate_cli.render
documentation: Provide helpers to render output for users.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.render.terminal_message
documentation:
Print a nicely formatted message as output to the user using a `rich` `Panel`.

Args:

: message: The message to print out.
  subject: An optional subject line to add in the header of the `Panel`.
  color: An optional color to style the `subject` header with. Defaults to "green".
  footer: An optional message to display in the footer of the `Panel`.
  indent: Adds padding to the left of the message. Defaults to True.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.render.render_json
documentation: Print nicely formatted representation of a JSON serializable python primitive.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.render.render_list_results
documentation:
Render a list of result data items in a `rich` `Table`.

Args:

: ctx: The JobbergateContext. This is needed to detect if `full` or `raw` output is needed.
  envelope: A ListResponseEnvelope containing the data items.
  style_mapper: The style mapper that should be used to apply styles to the columns of the table.
  hidden_fields: Columns that should (if not using `full` mode) be hidden in the `Table` output.
  title: The title header to include above the `Table` output.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.render.render_dict
documentation:
Render a dictionary in a `rich` `Table` that shows the key and value of each item.

Args:

: data: The dictionary to render.
  title: The title header to include above the `Table` output.
  hidden_fields: Keys that should be hidden in the `Table` output.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.render.render_single_result
documentation:
Render a single data item in a `rich` `Table`.

Args:

: ctx: The JobbergateContext. This is needed to detect if `full` or `raw` output is needed.
  result: The data item to display. May be a dict or a pydantic model.
  hidden_fields: Rows that should (if not using `full` mode) be hidden in the `Table` output.
  title: The title header to include above the `Table` output.
  value_mappers: Mapping functions to change fields before rendering.

functions.name: jobbergate_cli.render.render_demo
documentation: Show the demo for the jobbergate-cli.

classes.name: jobbergate_cli.render.StyleMapper

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.render.StyleMapper.__init__
documentation: Initialize the StyleMapper.

methods.name: jobbergate_cli.render.StyleMapper.map_style
documentation: Map a column name from the table to display to the style that should be used to render it.

Provide a mapper that can set `rich` styles for rendered output of data tables and dicts.

The subapps have list endpoints that return sets of values. These are rendered as tables in
the output. The StyleMapper class provides a way to simply define styles that should be applied
to the columns of the table.

Example:

The following code will print a table where the columns are colored according to the style_mapper

% code-block: python
%
% style_mapper = StyleMapper(
%     a="bold green",
%     b="red",
%     c="blue",
% )
% envelope = dict(
%     results=[
%         dict(a=1, b=2, c=3),
%         dict(a=4, b=5, c=6),
%         dict(a=7, b=8, c=9),
%     ],
%     pagination=dict(total=3)
% )
% render_list_results(jb_ctx, envelope, style_mapper)
